note: Trimmed local fallback for acpx-registry: verbatim entries (Claude, Codex, Gemini) copied from the official ACP registry on 2026-07-12, used only when the remote registry URL is unreachable. Schema is NOT acpx-invented -- it matches the official registry's own per-agent shape exactly, so the same parser handles both the remote fetch and this fallback. Re-sync periodically; entries go stale as upstream publishes new versions.

agents.name: Codex
agents.description: ACP adapter for OpenAI's coding assistant
agents.acpx_notes: Custom OPENAI_BASE_URL / litellm-compatible endpoint support not yet verified against this exact npm-published adapter -- open risk, see plan's Open Risks section.

install_semantics.npx: No acpx-side install step needed beyond having Node/npm on PATH -- npx resolves/caches/runs the package on demand. agents/install for an npx-only entry can be a no-op that just verifies Node/npm availability.
install_semantics.binary: Not exercised by claude/codex/gemini as of this snapshot (all npx-only in the official registry). Pick the distribution.binary key matching the host os-arch, download+extract 'archive' into ~/.acpx/adapters/<id>/, spawn 'cmd' with 'args'.
install_semantics.uvx: Same on-demand idea as npx, via the uv Python tool. Not used by claude/codex/gemini as of this snapshot.

acpx_meta.acpx_provider_hint: Non-standard field added by acpx on top of the official schema -- maps a registry entry to this plan's provider config model (openai/anthropic/gemini/litellm). Not present in the upstream registry.json; stripped/ignored if this file is ever diffed against a fresh upstream pull.
